columns.name: person_id
columns.description: unique identifier that joins this table to other tables

columns.name: gender_source_value
columns.description: gender of patient. Distinct values in data: 'male', 'female' (always lowercase). Use op='=' with these exact lowercase values.

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns.description: year the patient was born. ALWAYS requires joining the person table — never reference year_of_birth without including person in the FROM clause. To calculate age at diagnosis use: YEAR(co.condition_start_date) - p.year_of_birth. For early-onset filter use: YEAR(co.condition_start_date) - p.year_of_birth <= 49. For 5-year age groups use: CAST(FLOOR((YEAR(co.condition_start_date) - p.year_of_birth) / 5) * 5 AS INTEGER) AS age_group_start.

columns.name: race_source_value
columns.description: race/ethnicity of patient as a lowercase text label. Distinct values in data: 'chinese', 'malay', 'indian', 'filipino', 'bangladeshi', 'indonesian', 'indonesian chinese', 'vietnamese', 'other races'. Always use lowercase when filtering. Use op='=' for exact match or op='like' for partial match.

condition_occurrence.description: Condition of patient — one row per cancer diagnosis

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: condition_occurrence_id
columns.description: primary key (e.g. 'dx56418')

columns.name: condition_start_date
columns.description: date of diagnosis. When filtering for a specific year, always generate TWO filters: one with >= 'YYYY-01-01' and one with <= 'YYYY-12-31'. Only use a single bound for filtering before or after a certain year.

columns.name: condition_source_value
columns.description: Use ONLY for histology/tumour type filtering, NOT for cancer site filtering (use ICD10 for that). Format: topo_code||topo_description||histo_code||histo_description e.g. 'C18.3||Hepatic flexure||81403.0||Adenocarcinoma, nos'. For histology filtering use op='like' with these exact patterns: '%81403.0%' for adenocarcinoma NOS, '%84803.0%' for mucinous adenocarcinoma, '%84903.0%' for signet ring cell carcinoma, '%82633.0%' for adenocarcinoma in tubulovillous adenoma. NEVER use this column to filter cancer site or colorectal cancer cohort.

columns.name: ICD10
columns.description: PRIMARY column for cancer site/type filtering. ALWAYS use this column when the question asks about cancer type, cancer site, or diagnosis. Values in dataset: 'C18.0' (cecum), 'C18.2' (ascending colon), 'C18.3' (hepatic flexure), 'C18.4' (transverse colon), 'C18.5' (splenic flexure), 'C18.6' (descending colon), 'C18.7' (sigmoid colon), 'C18.9' (colon NOS), 'C19' (rectosigmoid), 'C20' (rectum). For all colorectal cancer use op='in' with value=['C18.0','C18.2','C18.3','C18.4','C18.5','C18.6','C18.7','C18.9','C19','C20']. For colon only use C18.x and C19. For rectum use op='=' with value='C20'. When asked about cancer types or how many cancer types exist, always GROUP BY ICD10 and return COUNT(DISTINCT person_id) per ICD10 code rather than a single COUNT(DISTINCT ICD10).

procedure_occurrence.description: Surgery and radiotherapy procedures. Surgical rows have procedure_concept_id=NULL; radiotherapy rows have procedure_concept_id set. To distinguish: procedure_occurrence_id starting with 'procedure_sx_' = surgery; 'procedure_rt_' = radiotherapy.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: procedure_occurrence_id
columns.description: primary key. Surgical rows use prefix 'procedure_sx_'; radiotherapy rows use 'procedure_rt_'.

columns.name: institution
columns.description: hospital/institution where the procedure was performed. Distinct values in data: 'SGH' (Singapore General Hospital), 'SKH' (Sengkang General Hospital), 'KKH' (KK Women's and Children's Hospital), 'CGH' (Changi General Hospital), 'NHC' (National Heart Centre). Empty string means institution not recorded.

columns.name: procedure_date
columns.description: date of the procedure in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use this for date filtering (preferred over procedure_datetime for date comparisons).

columns.name: procedure_datetime
columns.description: start date and time of procedure in DD/MM/YYYY HH:MM format.

columns.name: procedure_end_date
columns.description: end date of the procedure in YYYY-MM-DD format.

columns.name: procedure_end_datetime
columns.description: end date and time of procedure.

columns.name: procedure_concept_id
columns.description: concept ID of procedure. NULL for surgical procedures; non-NULL for radiotherapy.

columns.name: procedure_source_value
columns.description: free-text description of the surgery or radiotherapy technique.

columns.name: TOSP
columns.description: Table of Surgical Procedures code used for billing classification in Singapore. e.g. 'SB805A'. Use LIKE to filter by procedure category.

columns.name: intent
columns.description: treatment intent for radiotherapy rows. Distinct values in data: 'Curative', 'Palliative'. Empty string for surgical rows. When filtering, use op='like' with '%Curative%' or '%Palliative%' to handle trailing whitespace in source data.

columns.name: delivered_dose
columns.description: total radiation dose delivered in Gray (Gy). Only populated for radiotherapy rows.

columns.name: delivered_fraction
columns.description: number of radiotherapy fractions delivered. Only populated for radiotherapy rows.

measurement_mutation.description: EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) table storing staging, mutation, grade, and laterality measurements. Each row is one measurement type for one patient encounter. ALWAYS filter measurement_concept_name first to select the measurement type before reading value_as_concept_name.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: measurement_id
columns.description: primary key (e.g. 'meas_00123')

columns.name: measurement_concept_name
columns.description: Type of measurement. ALWAYS add a WHERE filter on this column. All 14 distinct values in data: 'BRAF Mutation Conclusion', 'KRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'NRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'APC Mutation Conclusion', 'Grade', 'Laterality', 'TNM Clin Stage Group', 'TNM Clin T', 'TNM Clin N', 'TNM Clin M', 'TNM Path Stage Group', 'TNM Path T', 'TNM Path N', 'TNM Path M'. NEVER use short names like 'KRAS' or 'BRAF' — always use full names like 'KRAS Mutation Conclusion'. For staging questions: ALWAYS use BOTH 'TNM Path Stage Group' AND 'TNM Clin Stage Group' together with a COALESCE pattern — prefer path stage per patient, fall back to clin stage if null. Use a CTE to get one stage per patient first, then aggregate. Never filter one stage type alone for a general staging question. For mutation questions: use op='in' with full names e.g. value=['KRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'BRAF Mutation Conclusion', 'NRAS Mutation Conclusion']. Always also filter value_as_concept_name = 'mutation detected' unless the question asks about both detected and not detected. For 'among patients who were tested' questions: denominator = patients who have any record in measurement_mutation for that mutation concept name regardless of result — NOT all colorectal patients. For grade use 'Grade', for laterality use 'Laterality'.

columns.name: measurement_date
columns.description: date the measurement was recorded. Use for time-based filtering of mutation or staging results.

columns.name: value_as_concept_name
columns.description: result of the measurement. Stage values are substages: 'I', 'II', 'IIA', 'IIB', 'IIC', 'IIIA', 'IIIB', 'IIIC', 'IVA', 'IVB', 'IVC', 'Stage Unknown'. To group into Roman numeral stages use CASE WHEN raw_stage = 'I' THEN 'I' WHEN raw_stage LIKE 'III%' THEN 'III' WHEN raw_stage LIKE 'II%' THEN 'II' WHEN raw_stage LIKE 'IV%' THEN 'IV' ELSE 'Unknown' END. Note: TNM Clin Stage Group only has values I, III, IIIB, IIIC, IVA, IVB, IVC, Stage Unknown — it is missing II and some substages. Mutation values: 'mutation detected', 'no mutation detected'. Grade values: 'Grade I', 'Grade II', 'Grade III', 'Grade/differentiation unknown, not stated, or not applicable'.

columns.name: value_source_value
columns.description: raw source value of the measurement result before standardisation. Useful for free-text or non-standardised values.

columns.name: measurement_event_id
columns.description: foreign key linking to procedure_occurrence_id of the associated procedure encounter

death.description: Death records for patients in the dataset. A record here means the patient has died — it does NOT mean they died from cancer specifically. cause_source_value may or may not be populated. For mortality questions: ALWAYS define the cancer patient cohort first using condition_occurrence, then LEFT JOIN death onto the cohort. This counts patients who died regardless of cause. Do NOT filter death_date by diagnosis year — apply date filters only to condition_start_date. For 'deaths among colorectal cancer patients': denominator = total colorectal patients in cohort, numerator = those with a non-null death_date. CRITICAL: the deaths CTE should SELECT directly FROM death table WHERE death_date IS NOT NULL — do NOT join person inside the deaths CTE. If user asks specifically about deaths CAUSED BY colorectal cancer, use cause_source_value filter and warn that this field may be incomplete.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person and to the cancer cohort CTE

columns.name: death_date
columns.description: date of death. NULL means patient is still alive (or status unknown). To filter for deceased patients use: death_date IS NOT NULL. Do NOT filter by year range — only use IS NOT NULL check.

columns.name: cause_source_value
columns.description: cause of death as recorded in source data. May be NULL or incomplete — only use this field if user explicitly asks about cause of death.

drug_exposure_cancerdrugs.description: Cancer drug/chemotherapy treatments. One row per drug prescription episode.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: drug_exposure_id
columns.description: primary key

columns.name: drug_source_value
columns.description: prescribed drug identifier combining NDC code and Gemscript code. Use ATC column for drug class filtering; use this column for specific drug name filtering.

columns.name: ATC
columns.description: ATC (Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical) classification code for the drug. Use for filtering by drug class (e.g. op='like' with '%L01%' for antineoplastic agents, '%L01XA%' for platinum compounds, '%L01BC%' for fluoropyrimidines like capecitabine/5-FU).

columns.name: drug_strength
columns.description: dose strength of the drug as prescribed (e.g. '500mg', '25mg').

columns.name: drug_form
columns.description: formulation/dosage form of the drug (e.g. 'Tablet', 'Injection', 'Infusion', 'Solution').

columns.name: route_source_value
columns.description: route of administration. Distinct values in data: 'IV' (intravenous), 'PO' (oral), 'CIV' (continuous IV infusion), 'IVINTER' (intermittent IV), 'IV Intermi' (intermittent IV), 'IVP' (IV push), 'IVCONT' (continuous IV), 'IV BOLUS', 'IM' (intramuscular). Empty string means not recorded.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: quantity of drug dispensed per prescription episode.

columns.name: case_type
columns.description: clinical case type for the drug episode. Distinct values in data: 'Outpatient', 'Inpatient'. Empty string means not recorded.

columns.name: drug_exposure_start_date
columns.description: start date of the drug prescription/administration.

columns.name: drug_exposure_end_date
columns.description: end date of the drug prescription/administration.

columns.name: institution
columns.description: hospital/institution where the drug was prescribed. Distinct values in data: 'NCC' (National Cancer Centre Singapore), 'SGH' (Singapore General Hospital), 'CGH' (Changi General Hospital).
